title: Understand: global winds and currents
description: Check your understanding of global winds and currents in this set of free, standards-aligned practice questions.
topic_path: Middle school Earth and space science - NGSS

assessment_items.question_content:
**What causes winds in the atmosphere?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the uneven heating of Earth’s surface by the sun
the gravitational pull of the moon on Earth
the movement of waves across Earth’s oceans

assessment_items.question_content:
**What causes air currents in the atmosphere?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the ocean absorbing excess heat from Earth’s atmosphere
the moon exerting a gravitational pull on Earth
the sun heating Earth’s surface unevenly

assessment_items.question_content:
**What causes air currents in the atmosphere?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: gravity from the moon pulling on Earth
ocean currents moving heat around Earth

assessment_items.question_content:
Air and water currents traveling long distances around Earth appear to move in curved rather than straight paths. This is known as the Coriolis effect.

**What is the main cause of the Coriolis effect?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Winds and ocean currents traveling long distances around Earth appear to move in curved rather than straight paths. This is known as the Coriolis effect.

**What is the main cause of the Coriolis effect?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Atmospheric and ocean currents traveling long distances around Earth appear to move in curved rather than straight paths. This is known as the Coriolis effect.

**What is the main cause of the Coriolis effect?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the rotation of Earth on its axis
the revolution of Earth around the sun

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best option to complete the sentence.**

>Ocean water is more dense when it is \_\_\_\_\_\_.

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Choose the best option to complete the sentence.**

>Ocean water is more dense when it is \_\_\_\_\_\_.

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Choose the best option to complete the sentence.**

>Ocean water is less dense when it is \_\_\_\_\_\_.

[[☃ radio 1]]

**How do ocean gyres redistribute heat around Earth?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They transport warm water from the poles to the equator.
They transport warm water from the equator to the poles.
They transport warm water between the two poles.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How do ocean gyres redistribute heat around Earth?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They carry warm water from the polar to the tropical regions.
They carry warm water from the tropical to the polar regions.
They carry warm water between the two polar regions.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How do ocean gyres redistribute heat around Earth?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They move warm water to the poles from the tropics.
They move warm water to the tropics from the poles.
They move warm water between the two poles.
